items.title: If this session has no `tero__*` tools
items.summary: Sessions only pick up MCP servers registered before launch (or after /mcps → r). Quick path:

items.title: 1) One-time: sibling package + index (adjust GIT_PARENT)
items.summary: export GITPARENT="${GITPARENT:-$HOME/git}"

items.title: 2) Register (user scope → ~/.grok/config.toml)
items.summary: grok mcp add tero \

items.title: 3) Verify, then new session (or /mcps → r if already inside TUI)
items.summary: grok mcp doctor tero   # handshake OK, 9 tools

items.title: cabal-devmelopner — Development Phases
items.summary: This document tracks the phased development of cabal-devmelopner.

items.title: PoC Phase (Current)
items.summary: Goal: Validate core architecture and get a working, extensible agent.

items.title: Deliverables
items.summary: - [x] Project skeleton + installable CLI

items.title: Recent Wave Updates (W2 Facade + Integration, 2026-07-09)
items.summary: As part of wsfull PR process:

items.title: Exit Criteria
items.summary: PoC not exited until P0 entrypoint is fixed and testing/stabilization is honest about remaining partials.

items.title: MVP Phase
items.summary: Goal: Make cabal-devmelopner a practical daily co-dev tool.

items.title: Key Features
items.summary: - Full-featured TUI (status, progress, logs, task management)

items.title: Exit Criteria
items.summary: - Comfortable to use for real development work.

items.title: Production Phase
items.summary: Goal: Robust, scalable, multi-agent development system.

items.title: Key Features
items.summary: - Multi-provider support (xAI, Claude, others)

items.title: Exit Criteria
items.summary: - Can reliably run long development sessions with minimal human intervention.

items.title: Notes
items.summary: - All phases should maintain the core principles: repo-agnostic, extensible communications, strong Tero integration, and clean architecture.

items.title: Appended: W2 + C0 fix for PR#12 (2026-07-09)
items.summary: - W2 facade + AgentDomain + StructuredResponse wired + C0 error emit fixed (agent.py + test green).

items.title: cabal-devmelopner
items.summary: cabal-devmelopner is a repo-agnostic development agent designed for long-running, high-quality coding assistance.

items.title: Features (PoC)
items.summary: - Uses Grok via the raw xAI API

items.title: Setup (Recommended: UV)
items.summary: This project uses uv for Python version management, dependency resolution, and running.

items.title: One-command setup (Ubuntu / WSL / macOS / Linux)
items.summary: git clone https://github.com/tzervas/cabal-devmelopner.git

items.title: Manual setup
items.summary: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

items.title: 1. Install uv if you don't have it
items.summary: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

items.title: 2. Clone and enter the repo
items.summary: cd cabal-devmelopner

items.title: 3. Sync environment
items.summary: uv sync --all-extras

items.summary: Tero is opt-in. Defaults expect sibling checkouts next to this repo:

items.title: Tero-MCP integration
items.summary: [Tero](https://github.com/tzervas) is a Layer-1 corpus index query surface: cited lookups over docs, decisions, issues, changelog entries, and skills. cabal-de…

items.title: What Tero is (and is not)
items.summary: Treat results as pointers: open the cited path and read the source. Do not treat summaries as ground truth.

items.title: Expected sibling layout
items.summary: Defaults assume a shared parent directory (e.g. ~/git or /root/git):

items.title: Prerequisites
items.summary: 1. Python ≥ 3.11 for tero-mcp-lite (this project targets 3.14 via uv).
